_note: PLACEHOLDER jab copy — rewrite later. 8 lines per tier, player-directed, in the mockup's voice (playful, sports-commentator, a little mean). The reveal picks one per tier deterministically via challenge.number % 8, so every player sees the same line on the same day. Keep lines generic across all four formats — no hardcoded numbers, since they're shared by Crowd Crunch, Odd One In, Split or Steal, and Herd Meter alike.
mastermind: The crowd tried to out-think each other. You out-thought the out-thinking.
The herd zigged, the galaxy-brains zagged, and you were already standing exactly where you needed to be.
Somewhere, a game theory professor just felt a disturbance.
You didn't beat the herd. You *herded* it.
Everyone else was still doing the math. You were already done.
The crowd spent all night overthinking this. You spent four seconds.
This is the part where we admit nobody saw you coming.
Somewhere, a spreadsheet just closed itself out of respect.
sharp: Two steps ahead of the crowd. The third step is where the gold lives.
Close enough to smell the jackpot. Not close enough to taste it.
The one-step thinkers salute you from several ranks down.
Sharp, not psychic. We'll allow it.
You read the room. You just didn't read the whole room.
One more level of thinking and this was yours.
Respectable instincts. Slightly less respectable follow-through.
The top of the herd still counts as the herd. Barely.
mid: Perfectly average. The crowd you were trying to outguess? You're in it. You're load-bearing.
If everyone thought like you, the target would be exactly where you didn't look.
Not a sheep. Not a mastermind. A guy.
You are the control group in someone else's experiment.
Statistically speaking, you are what "statistically speaking" means.
Congratulations on your role as human ballast.
The bell curve says hello. You're the bell.
You didn't lose. You also didn't do the thing that isn't losing.
sheep: You picked what everyone else was already picking. So did everyone else.
Baa. That's it. That's the feedback.
The crowd didn't outguess you — you WERE the guess.
One step of thinking. The game asked for two. Maybe tomorrow.
You followed the herd so closely you should get a cut of its winnings. You won't.
Predictable is a strong word. We're using it anyway.
The good news: you're never alone. The bad news: that's the whole problem.
Somewhere, a shepherd is very proud of you.
npc: We checked the logs twice. This really happened.
You didn't just miss the target. You missed the chart.
This result has been forwarded to absolutely no one, but it was close.
Somewhere, a random number generator is offended you did worse than it would have.
Today's strategy: vibes. The vibes were wrong.
We've alerted no one, but we thought about it.
The chart had to be extended to fit your pick. Congratulations?
Outguessed by the people who fell asleep on their keyboards.
